publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, Financial Records, Legal Documents, Photographs
did.abstract: Office correspondence, bills, receipts, contracts, job indexes, photographs, blueprints, and detail drawings

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence consists of both outgoing (51 letter impression books, 1899-1926) and incoming letters (131 letterboxes, ca. 1892-ca. 1931) to and from clients, contractors, and supply companies in regard to the design and construction of schools, churches, private homes, apartment houses, commercial establishments, hospitals, public buildings, and other structures in New York, New Jersey, and Pennsylvania, including the stairs and stone walls at Watkins Glen (1908), the Arnot Art Gallery, the Corning Glass Works, Cortland County and Arnot-Ogden Hospitals, the Elmira College Library, Elmira Y.M.C.A. and Masonic Temple, and the Elmira Free Academy; also, bills, receipts, contracts, indexes (2 volumes) of jobs, and various photographs, blueprints, and detail drawings.

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series I. 51 Letterbooks - Copies of Outgoing Correspondence, 1899 - 1926 Volumes 16-22, 24-65, 68
Series II. Incoming letters, bills, receipts and related items Boxes 1-131

Series III. Job file drawings, #5-1791 Box 133-148, 153; Mapcase Folders 1-18 This series is arranged alphabetically by the location of the job: first by state, then by city, and finally by the name of the project.Plans for related buildings have often been grouped together.

p.ref: 1. Roselle Park, NJ; Albany - Eddytown, NY

arrangement.p: Series IV. Recent correspondence, Bickford genealogy, photographs, printed material Box 149
Series V. Job Index, Photographs, Cartoons etc. Box 150
Series VI. Glass slides, Printing blocks, Glass plate negatives, etc. Box 132, 151

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Pierce and Bickford records, #2299. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Started in 1891, Pierce and Bickford was an architectural firm located in Elmira, N.Y. made up of Hiram H. Bickford (1855-1932) and Joseph H. Pierce (1863-1929). Joseph Pierce was the senior partner of the two, having worked as an architect in Elmira since 1880. He had worked with Otis Dockstander from 1883-1890 but the pair dissolved their partnership shortly before Pierce joined with Bickford. Little is known about Bickford before he came to work for Pierce and Dockstander, except that he had worked as a draftsperson at a number of firms in the Northeast. Pierce & Bickford designed hundreds of buildings together - residential, religious, commercial and civic throughout Central New York and nearby Pennsylvania.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fifty-one MSS. volumes of letterbooks containing copies of the outgoing correspondence of the architectural firm of Pierce & Bickford of Elmira, New York.Includes letters to clients,as well as to contractors and supply companies, in regard to the design and construction of buildings in New York, Pennsylvania, and New Jersey. Among the outstanding jobs are:

did.unittitle: Church and Main Streets, Elmira, for Mrs. Julia Reynolds. See correspondence in Volume (Letterbook) 17
National Protective Legion Building, Waverly, N. Y.
Y. M. C. A, Geneva, N. Y.
Letterbook 67 marked "Arnot Art Gallery", Bills paid

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note on cover: From July 17, 1913: Bills paid

did.unittitle: Incoming letters, bills, receipts and related items

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The numbers in parentheses refer to numbers sometimes written on the backs of the letterboxes

did.unittitle: Letters, many related to processes for strengthening steel (marked 1896 H.H.B.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains a contract dated December 1896

p.emph: Eberle, J.F., Tannery (also referred to as the factory Building)

did.unittitle: Recent correspondence, Bickford genealogy, photographs, printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 vol. "Job Index": Vol. I Jobs 1-1612 ; Vol. I1 Jobs 1613-1792; Photographs of schools and banks; Scattered newspaper articles,1909,1911,1950's-1966; Cartoons of H. H. Bickford and J. H. Pierce by Zim (?); Map of Chemung County,1911; Plan of Elmira College campus (undated).

did.unittitle: Glass slides, Printing blocks, Glass plate negatives, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains the wood block of Clifton Springs Sanitarium (see prints in Box 143)

did.unittitle: Ledger with list of photographs & negatives; Contents of envelopes

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Note on folder states that these were pulled from box with glass slides.
